content: Just as the educational definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive practice for language learners to refine their writing and comprehension by looking back on their own outputs, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate structured, graded reflexion exercises modeled on high school writing workshops to improve their output consistency. This means instead of only prompting LLMs to critique bad solutions post-hoc, we can have them iteratively revise their responses following the same scaffolded reflection prompts used to teach human students critical thinking.
